title: Empirical Kelly position sizing
why: Gaussian Kelly `f* = μ/σ²` overstates leverage when OOF PnL has a heavy left tail. Meta-filtered series usually do, because the meta filters out winners more aggressively than losers in some regimes. Empirical Kelly computes growth-optimal leverage directly from the historical PnL distribution rather than assuming Gaussian — automatically more conservative when skew is negative.
scope: new `pipeline/sizing.py` with `empirical_kelly(pnl_series) -> float`; report alongside Gaussian Kelly in `summary.json`. Does not change live trading; informational only.

history.reason: Self-contained pure-function ticket (empirical vs Gaussian Kelly). Implementable + testable now without a survivor; Paso 2 / Loop B sizing prereq. Informational-only in summary.json per scope.

New pipeline/sizing.py: gaussian_kelly (mu/sigma^2, clamped>=0), empirical_kelly (maximize mean(log(1+f*r)) via scipy bounded minimize, no-bankruptcy bound f<1/|worst_loss|, cap when no losses), kelly_sizing combined block (+half-kelly +skew +empirical/gaussian ratio). Wired into summary.json[kelly_sizing] on best-model taken-trade net pnl. Informational only — not wired to live sizing per scope. Empirical < Gaussian under negative skew (the motivating case) verified by test. Tests: tests/test_sizing.py (9). 581/581 pass.
feat(B0001): Gaussian vs empirical Kelly sizing diagnostics
